description: Vendor-agnostic connectivity We tunnel to PLCs (Siemens, Beckhoff), HMIs, and IP cameras through the same portal, simplifying our OT landscape. We love it
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - Top-tier security without VPN headaches Built-in two-factor authentication, role-based permissions, and complete audit logs mean external partners reach only the machines they’re authorised for—no manual firewall tweaking required. - Lightning-fast deployment We added the SiteManager to our network in under 30 minutes. Plug-and-play setup avoided any production downtime. - Centralised device management in GateManager Firmware updates, certificate handling, and user rights are all handled through a single cloud dashboard—eliminating weekly on-site visits.
cons: - Somewhat complex licensing model Combining GateManager user packages, SiteManager units, and device slots takes a bit of up-front guidance. - UI feels dated Functionally solid, but not a slick modern web app—menus get cramped on smaller screens.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Gives total control over network and hardware monitoring. Real-time notification is quite helpful to track problem in instantly.
author: David B.
pros: Remote access issues with OT equipment is solved by Secomea, integration is easy and give total control over IOT equipment and factory components. Remote monitoring is the best features, it help to gives access for engineer to troubleshoot error from anywhere. Detailed logs and history to supervise machine access history and activity for auditing.
cons: The vulnerability hubs is not user-friendly and many of the operation is still need for manual interruption to check for each error.

description: Satisfaying with great features for remote access
author: Morris K.
pros: Consumer friendly especially for the IT team
cons: Sometimes there can be occasional disconnection.

description: Overall very good we have had no issues with the hardware and the support from the local distributor (DAANET) has always been quick to resolve any problems.
author: Scott N.
pros: The large library of pre-configured devices available including ethernet and USB devices that work with minimal additional setup. I also liked being able to drag and drop users to limit/grant access to subsets of the equipment.
cons: Setup for remote access to industrial devices is very good but I did have difficulty trying to use the device to allow windows updates to a PC for a short time window. Would also be good to be able to add custom devices to be saved to an account we use a number of Kingfisher RTUs that are configured just by allowing the relevant ports.
